findings.issue: Theme.Sanctum inherits from the framework theme `android:Theme.Material.Light.NoActionBar`, which is the legacy (pre-Lollipop Holo-Material) framework theme and is Light-only. Task 7.md 'Details' block (line 138) explicitly requires `parent="Theme.Material3.DayNight.NoActionBar"`, and UX guidelines (ux-guidelines.md §Design System) mandate 'Material 3 as base. Dark-first.' The current parent (a) is not Material 3, (b) ignores system dark mode during the pre-Compose splash/inflation window, and (c) is not guaranteed to expose the AppCompat/Material3 attributes that the Compose activity theme overlay expects when DynamicColors are active on S+. This is a direct deviation from the spec.
findings.fix: Change themes.xml parent to `Theme.Material3.DayNight.NoActionBar` (requires `com.google.android.material:material` which should already be on the classpath via Compose Material3 BOM; if not, add it to :app/build.gradle.kts). Example: `<style name="Theme.Sanctum" parent="Theme.Material3.DayNight.NoActionBar" />`.

findings.issue: Inline hardcoded English string `Text("Sanctum loading…")` in Compose. UX guidelines (ux-guidelines.md §Copy Reference) state 'No inline hardcoded strings in Compose'. The string is also English despite UI-language policy 'Russian for all user-facing text'. Task spec acknowledges this as a temporary stub to be replaced by `SanctumApp()` in Task 10, but there is no TODO marker in the code pointing to that fact, which makes follow-up easy to miss in reviews.
findings.fix: Either (a) add an explicit `// TODO(Task 10): replace stub with SanctumApp() NavHost` comment immediately above the Box, or (b) reuse the already-canonical `R.string.chat_loading_model` ('Загрузка модели…') via `stringResource(R.string.chat_loading_model)` so no hardcoded copy leaks. Option (a) is sufficient for Phase 1 bootstrap.

findings.issue: `android:icon="@android:drawable/sym_def_app_icon"` uses a framework placeholder icon and there is no `android:roundIcon` attribute. Acceptable for a Phase 1 bootstrap APK that just needs to launch, but it leaves the user-visible launcher entry looking like a generic system icon and will produce an ugly fallback on launchers that expect roundIcon. Not a blocker for Task 7 scope (icons are not in acceptance criteria).
findings.fix: Track as Phase 5 polish item. Optionally add a real `@mipmap/ic_launcher` (and `ic_launcher_round`) resource before first dog-food build; not required to close Task 7.

summary: Hilt wiring (@HiltAndroidApp + @AndroidEntryPoint), POST_NOTIFICATIONS SDK-33 gating with registerForActivityResult registered as a field before setContent, SanctumTheme composable shape, manifest TAC-7/TAC-8/TAC-12/TAC-14 (6 permissions, SystemForegroundService with foregroundServiceType=dataSync+tools:node=merge, allowBackup/fullBackupContent/usesCleartextTraffic hardening, MainActivity FQN plumbing into DefaultDownloadRepository.mainActivityFqn), uses-native-library entries for libOpenCL.so and libvndksupport.so with required=false, and the strings.xml canonical set (exactly 22 keys, all names/values verbatim with correct positional placeholders and U+2026/U+00B7 unicode) are all correct. Cross-file consistency verified: DefaultDownloadRepository.mainActivityFqn exists as @Volatile companion var in core-runtime/.../DownloadRepository.kt:81 and the import path app.sanctum.machina.core.data.DefaultDownloadRepository is valid. One high-severity deviation: themes.xml parent is `android:Theme.Material.Light.NoActionBar` (framework Holo-Material, Light-only) instead of the spec-required `Theme.Material3.DayNight.NoActionBar` — this contradicts both task 7.md Details line 138 and ux-guidelines.md 'Material 3 as base, Dark-first'. Two low-severity items (stub copy has no TODO marker; launcher icon is a framework default placeholder).
